headline: These Four Stocks Could Do What Cisco Did in 2000
summary: In March of 2000, Cisco Systems Inc. (CSCO) briefly became the most valuable company in the world. Not Walmart Inc. (WMT). Not General Electric Co. (GE). Not Exxon Mobil Corp. (XOM). At the time, a lot of people thought that was absurd. After all, Cisco wasn’t a consumer brand. It didn’t make cars. It didn’t drill for oil. Most people couldn’t have told you exactly what the company did.InvestorPlace - Stock Market News, Stock Advice & Trading Tips But Wall Street understood something important.
